tool_lists.name: cp

It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Copy a file or directory from one location to another. tool_lists.name: find
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Find any file or directories under specific path that contain name in its file name. name.description: The name of the file or directory to search for. If None, all items are returned.

It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Find any file or directories under specific path that contain name in its file name.  This method searches for files of any extension and directories within a specified path that match the given name. If no name is provided, it returns all files and directories in the specified path and its subdirectories. Note: This method performs a recursive search through all subdirectories of the given path. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def find(path: string = '.', name: string = 'None') -> Any: pass

path.description: The directory path to start the search. Defaults to the current directory (".").

name.description: The name of the file or directory to search for. If None, all items are returned.

tool_lists.name: grep

It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Search for lines in a file of any extension at current directory that contain the specified pattern. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def grep(file_name: string, pattern: string) -> Any: pass

file_name.description: The name of the file to search. No path is allowed and you can only perform on file at local directory.

tool_lists.name: ls

tool_lists.prototype: def ls(a: boolean = False) -> Any: pass

a.description: Show hidden files and directories. Defaults to False.

tool_lists.name: mv
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Move a file or directory from one location to another. so Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def mv(source: string, destination: string) -> Any: pass

source.description: Source name of the file or directory to move. Source must be local to the current directory.

destination.description: The destination name to move the file or directory to. Destination must be local to the current directory and cannot be a path. If destination is not an existing directory like when renaming something, destination is the new file name.

It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Display the last part of a file of any extension. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def tail(file_name: string, lines: integer = 10) -> Any: pass

file_name.description: The name of the file to display. No path is allowed and you can only perform on file at local directory.

lines.description: The number of lines to display from the end of the file. Defaults to 10.

tool_lists.name: touch

It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Create a new file of any extension in the current directory. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

file_name.description: The name of the new file in the current directory. file_name is local to the current directory and does not allow path.

tool_lists.name: wc
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Count the number of lines, words, and characters in a file of any extension from current directory. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def wc(file_name: string, mode: string = 'l') -> Any: pass

file_name.description: Name of the file of current directory to perform wc operation on.

mode.description: Mode of operation ('l' for lines, 'w' for words, 'c' for characters).

tool_lists.name: find
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. tool_lists.name: find

tool_lists.name: find
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. name.description: The name of the file or directory to search for. If None, all items are returned.

It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: List the contents of the current directory. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def ls(a: boolean = False) -> Any: pass

a.description: Show hidden files and directories. Defaults to False.

tool_lists.name: mkdir
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Create a new directory in the current directory. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

dir_name.description: The name of the new directory at current directory. You can only create directory at current directory.

tool_lists.name: mv
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Move a file or directory from one location to another. so Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def mv(source: string, destination: string) -> Any: pass

source.description: Source name of the file or directory to move. Source must be local to the current directory.

destination.description: The destination name to move the file or directory to. Destination must be local to the current directory and cannot be a path. If destination is not an existing directory like when renaming something, destination is the new file name.

tool_lists.name: pwd
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Return the current working directory path. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

file_name.description: The name of the file or directory to remove.

tool_lists.name: rmdir
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Remove a directory at current directory. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

dir_name.description: The name of the directory to remove. Directory must be local to the current directory.

tool_lists.name: sort
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Sort the contents of a file line by line. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

file_name.description: The name of the file appeared at current directory to sort.

tool_lists.name: tail
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Display the last part of a file of any extension. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def tail(file_name: string, lines: integer = 10) -> Any: pass

file_name.description: The name of the file to display. No path is allowed and you can only perform on file at local directory.

lines.description: The number of lines to display from the end of the file. Defaults to 10.

tool_lists.name: touch
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Create a new file of any extension in the current directory. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

file_name.description: The name of the new file in the current directory. file_name is local to the current directory and does not allow path.

tool_lists.name: wc
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Count the number of lines, words, and characters in a file of any extension from current directory. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def wc(file_name: string, mode: string = 'l') -> Any: pass

file_name.description: Name of the file of current directory to perform wc operation on.

mode.description: Mode of operation ('l' for lines, 'w' for words, 'c' for characters).

tool_lists.name: cp
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def cp(source: string, destination: string) -> Any: pass

source.description: The name of the file or directory to copy.

destination.description: The destination name to copy the file or directory to. If the destination is a directory, the source will be copied into this directory. No file paths allowed.

tool_lists.name: echo

tool_lists.prototype: def echo(content: string, file_name: string = 'None') -> Any: pass

content.description: The content to write or display.

file_name.description: The name of the file at current directory to write the content to. Defaults to None.

tool_lists.name: find
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Find any file or directories under specific path that contain name in its file name.  This method searches for files of any extension and directories within a specified path that match the given name. If no name is provided, it returns all files and directories in the specified path and its subdirectories. Note: This method performs a recursive search through all subdirectories of the given path. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def find(path: string = '.', name: string = 'None') -> Any: pass

path.description: The directory path to start the search. Defaults to the current directory (".").

name.description: The name of the file or directory to search for. If None, all items are returned.

It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Write content to a file at current directory or display it in the terminal. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def echo(content: string, file_name: string = 'None') -> Any: pass

content.description: The content to write or display.

file_name.description: The name of the file at current directory to write the content to. Defaults to None.

tool_lists.name: find
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Find any file or directories under specific path that contain name in its file name.  This method searches for files of any extension and directories within a specified path that match the given name. If no name is provided, it returns all files and directories in the specified path and its subdirectories. Note: This method performs a recursive search through all subdirectories of the given path. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def find(path: string = '.', name: string = 'None') -> Any: pass

path.description: The directory path to start the search. Defaults to the current directory (".").

name.description: The name of the file or directory to search for. If None, all items are returned.

tool_lists.prototype: def grep(file_name: string, pattern: string) -> Any: pass

file_name.description: The name of the file to search. No path is allowed and you can only perform on file at local directory.

tool_lists.name: mv
tool_lists.description: This tool belongs to the Gorilla file system. It is a simple file system that allows users to perform basic file operations such as navigating directories, creating files and directories, reading and writing to files, etc. Tool description: Move a file or directory from one location to another. so Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
tool_lists.prototype: def mv(source: string, destination: string) -> Any: pass

source.description: Source name of the file or directory to move. Source must be local to the current directory.

destination.description: The destination name to move the file or directory to. Destination must be local to the current directory and cannot be a path. If destination is not an existing directory like when renaming something, destination is the new file name.
